concepts.name: Binary Search Tree
concepts.short_definition: A binary tree where for each node, all values in its left subtree are less than its value, and all values in its right subtree are greater.

concepts.name: Node
concepts.short_definition: A fundamental unit in a tree data structure, typically containing data and pointers to child nodes.

concepts.name: Root Node
concepts.short_definition: The topmost node in a tree, from which all other nodes descend.

concepts.name: Leaf Node
concepts.short_definition: A node with no children.

concepts.name: Parent Node
concepts.short_definition: A node that has one or more child nodes.

concepts.name: Child Node
concepts.short_definition: A node that is directly connected to another node (its parent) and descends from it.

concepts.name: Left Child
concepts.short_definition: A child node that is located to the left of its parent.

concepts.name: Right Child
concepts.short_definition: A child node that is located to the right of its parent.

concepts.name: Key
concepts.short_definition: The information stored within a node of a tree, used for comparison and ordering.

concepts.name: Insertion
concepts.short_definition: The process of adding a new node with a specific key into a data structure.

concepts.name: BST Insertion Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: The step-by-step procedure for adding a new node into a Binary Search Tree while maintaining its properties.

concepts.name: Comparison
concepts.short_definition: The act of comparing the key of a new node with the key of an existing node in the BST.

concepts.name: Traversal
concepts.short_definition: The process of visiting each node in a tree in a systematic order.

concepts.name: In-order Traversal
concepts.short_definition: A traversal method that visits nodes in ascending order of their keys in a BST.

concepts.name: Recursive Insertion
concepts.short_definition: Implementing the BST insertion algorithm using recursive function calls.

concepts.name: Iterative Insertion
concepts.short_definition: Implementing the BST insertion algorithm using loops and explicit state management.

concepts.name: Empty Tree
concepts.short_definition: A tree that contains no nodes.

concepts.name: Tree Height
concepts.short_definition: The number of edges on the longest path from the root to a leaf node.

concepts.name: Tree Depth
concepts.short_definition: The number of edges from the root to a specific node.

concepts.name: Balanced BST
concepts.short_definition: A BST where the heights of the left and right subtrees of any node differ by at most one.

concepts.name: Unbalanced BST
concepts.short_definition: A BST where the height difference between subtrees can be significant, potentially leading to performance degradation.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the computational resources (time) required by an algorithm as a function of the input size.

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the memory resources required by an algorithm as a function of the input size.

concepts.name: Worst-Case Insertion
concepts.short_definition: The scenario where BST insertion takes the longest time, typically when the tree becomes skewed.

concepts.name: Average-Case Insertion
concepts.short_definition: The typical time complexity for BST insertion, assuming a reasonably balanced tree.

concepts.name: Recursion
concepts.short_definition: A programming technique where a function calls itself to solve smaller instances of the same problem.

concepts.name: Iteration
concepts.short_definition: A programming technique that involves repeating a block of code multiple times, usually with loops.

concepts.name: Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A variable that stores the memory address of another variable or object.

concepts.name: Null Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer that does not point to any valid memory location.

concepts.name: Tree Structure
concepts.short_definition: The hierarchical arrangement of nodes in a tree data structure.

concepts.name: Binary Tree Properties
concepts.short_definition: The rules that define a binary tree, such as each node having at most two children.

concepts.name: BST Property
concepts.short_definition: The ordering rule for keys in a Binary Search Tree (left < parent < right).

concepts.name: Duplicate Keys
concepts.short_definition: When the same key value appears more than once in a BST.

concepts.name: Handling Duplicates
concepts.short_definition: Strategies for inserting duplicate keys into a BST, such as placing them in the right subtree or disallowing them.

concepts.name: Algorithm Design
concepts.short_definition: The process of creating a step-by-step procedure to solve a computational problem.

concepts.name: Data Structure Operations
concepts.short_definition: The fundamental actions that can be performed on a data structure, like insertion, deletion, and search.

concepts.name: Tree Visualization
concepts.short_definition: Representing a tree structure graphically to aid understanding.

concepts.name: Memory Allocation
concepts.short_definition: The process of reserving a block of computer memory for a program's use.

concepts.name: Function Call Stack
concepts.short_definition: A data structure used by compilers to keep track of active function calls during program execution, relevant for recursion.
